item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IN my opinion the idea was common and not something special but I appreciate your work so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple and pleasant, too much so I would say. There's nothing about the article that I can pick out as subpar, and the execution is competent, but the end product feels bland. I saw each storybeat coming from a mile away, and none of them had any impact on me. I'm going to have to novote on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yeah that was pretty vague criticism, sorry, I intended to say that the description broke rather unnaturally as there are two whole paragraphs before the details of the effect are explained further. The current structure is much better, and I've retracted my -1, but this sentence:

SCP-3913 will, if more than 2.56 kilometers from the corpse of Jedediah Phillips (hereafter referred to as SCP-3913-1), begin independent operation and move towards SCP-3913-1.

still feels clunky for me. Maybe it's more of a personal preference if anything, I don't know, but I would put the cause (if….) before the effect (SCP-3913 will….) instead of splitting the effect clause.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1: No significance to 2.56. I suppose the object just considers that to be close enough.
2: The tires and the mechanical parts are indeed invulnerable. That leaves the repair bit orphaned, now that I think about it. Removed.
3: Yes. They did. I thought about including that test even, along with an airplane and space based test but that didn't really jive with the emotion of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• Is there any significance to the 2.56 number?
  • The 'repair' comment in the first paragraph seems odd when it's apparent that the truck doesn't have invulnerable tires or methods of locomotion and thus couldn't theoretically move if it didn't have said methods repaired. That's super nitpicky though.
  • That 'terrain that would preclude non-anomalous vehicles' also comes out weird. What, did the Foundation have it try crossing the ocean or something?

But yeah, truthfully it's a perfectly written emotional article. The final addendum was also a nice touch, showing the potential this thing has for creepiness. Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate the comment, and certainly can't speak to what you "felt" about those paragraphs, but one is strictly descriptive whilst the other goes into significant detail.

Still, to improve the story flow I've moved the 4th paragraph to the 2nd along with a few minor edits. Now paragraph 1 dovetails into what was paragraph 4 more neatly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

It hurt my emotion bone.
In a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

The first and the fourth paragraph of the Description felt repetitive. It seems to be unintentional, but is a major oversight nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet. I like that. I was a little confused as to the operation, whether it had an anomalous effect whether it was closer or further than 2.whatever kilometers, but that could just be my own thickheadedness through no fault of the article. Appreciated the little details that gave this a lot of character. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang. That packed a solid punch for being so brief. I'm reminded of a less twisted version of Stephen King's "Christine."

Solid Job Cimmerian. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as emotional articles go, this hits all my favorite points. It's short, it's sweet, it's weird, and it's logical-ish. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah. This is a thing. Had an idea earlier today about a truck that was following a person around and ended up with a truck that misses its owner. I like it.

Image source here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1979_Kenworth_W900A_by_Pimlico_Badger.jpg
